findings.description: The HTML title/header is rendered as a single run-on block rather than being centered on separate lines as in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'SUMMARY OF NOTICE OF', 'INTENT TO REDEVELOP A BROWNFIELDS PROPERTY', 'Brownfields Property Name: Colwick Road Property', and 'Brownfields Project Number: 25095-21-060' each on separate centered lines. HTML collapses the first three into one long line and puts the project number on a second line, both left-aligned.

findings.description: The title block in the PDF is centered and bold; in the HTML it is left-aligned and bold.
findings.evidence: PDF: centered bold heading lines. HTML: left-aligned bold heading text.

findings.description: The contact address block for Bruce Nicholson is centered in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the closing address block (Bruce Nicholson, Chief / Brownfields Redevelopment Section / etc.) centered on the page. HTML renders this block as left-aligned paragraphs.

findings.description: The HTML has no heading elements (h1-h6) at all. The document title and section headers are rendered as bold text within paragraph tags rather than using proper heading hierarchy.
findings.evidence:
The PDF has a clear document title 'SUMMARY OF NOTICE OF INTENT TO REDEVELOP A BROWNFIELDS PROPERTY' with subtitle lines, but the HTML renders these as within

tags rather than as

or other heading elements.

findings.description: The footer reference '25095-21-060/Colwick Road Property/202500925' appears at the end of the HTML as a paragraph. While this text does appear in the PDF (as a header/footer), its inclusion is acceptable but slightly redundant since it appears as document metadata.
findings.evidence: The text '25095-21-060/Colwick Road Property/202500925' appears at the top of the PDF page as a header/footer and is reproduced at the bottom of the HTML content.
